import ray

content.text: The script comes from a ConfigMap `ray-job-script`. I'll update the ConfigMap, then restart the pod so it picks up the new content (subPath mounts don't auto-update).

input.command: kubectl -n ray get configmap ray-job-script -o jsonpath='{.data.job\.py}'
kubectl -n ray get svc ray-head -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports}'; echo

content.text: The service exposes GCS on 6379 (the client port 10001 isn't even available). I'll update the ConfigMap to use `ray-head:6379` and recreate the pod.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/cm-patch.json <<'EOF'
{"data":{"job.py":"import ray

ray.init(address="ray-head:6379")

print("pong")

ray.shutdown()
"}}
EOF
kubectl -n ray patch configmap ray-job-script --type merge --patch-file /tmp/cm-patch.json
kubectl -n ray get configmap ray-job-script -o jsonpath='{.data.job\.py}'

content.content:
configmap/ray-job-script patched
import ray

ray.init(address="ray-head:6379")

print("pong")

ray.shutdown()

tool_use_result.stdout:
configmap/ray-job-script patched
import ray

ray.init(address="ray-head:6379")

print("pong")

ray.shutdown()

content.text: Now I need the pod to pick up the new content. subPath mounts don't update live, so I'll recreate the pod.

input.command:
kubectl -n ray get pod ray-client -o yaml > /tmp/ray-client.yaml
kubectl -n ray delete pod ray-client --wait=true
kubectl -n ray apply -f /tmp/ray-client.yaml
kubectl -n ray wait --for=condition=Ready pod/ray-client --timeout=120s

content.text: The script now points to `ray-head:6379`. The remaining error is a stale local Ray session dir. Let me clean it and retry.

input.command: kubectl -n ray exec ray-client -- sh -c 'rm -rf /tmp/ray; python /opt/job.py'; echo "EXIT: $?"

content.text: The session name is dictated by the cluster's GCS — the driver pod isn't a cluster node, so no local raylet writes `node_ip_address.json`. Connecting a bare driver via GCS (6379) requires the host to be a Ray node. Let me verify whether the client pod can join the cluster as a node.

input.command: kubectl -n ray exec ray-client -- sh -c 'rm -rf /tmp/ray; ray start --address=ray-head:6379 2>&1 | head -40; echo "START EXIT: $?"'

content.content:
2026-06-18 02:08:27,974	WARNING services.py:1996 -- WARNING: The object store is using /tmp instead of /dev/shm because /dev/shm has only 67108864 bytes available. This will harm performance! You may be able to free up space by deleting files in /dev/shm. If you are inside a Docker container, you can increase /dev/shm size by passing '--shm-size=10.24gb' to 'docker run' (or add it to the run_options list in a Ray cluster config). Make sure to set this to more than 30% of available RAM.

content.text: A node started successfully. Now `node_ip_address.json` exists locally, so the job should connect. Let me run it.

content.text: It works and prints `pong` with exit 0. Let me confirm it's repeatable on a clean exec (the raylet persists in the pod):

input.command: kubectl -n ray exec ray-client -- python /opt/job.py 2>/dev/null; echo "EXIT: $?"
